summary: Deterministic proof for durable improve revision artifacts, checked-in improve builders/schemas/fixtures, and runtime-fingerprint policy behavior that observes model/runtime identity without pinning by default.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: improve artifact durability and runtime fingerprint policy
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the five listed improvement and runtime-fingerprint claims are marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Every future improve command shape is covered.
Runtime identity is inferred from human-oriented logs.
Default model/provider drift is a behavior regression.
Pinned runtime policy belongs to Cautilus instead of host adapters.
A runtime change directly edits consumer-owned prompts, skills, or adapter files.

protectedBehaviors: improve propose writes cautilus.improve_proposal.v1 with one bounded revision brief
revision artifacts preserve report context, intent profile, and target snapshot hash
runtime fingerprints normalize explicit telemetry into telemetry.runtimeFingerprint
prior evidence without runtime identity produces model_runtime_unobserved instead of a failure
runtime identity is not inferred from human-oriented logs
skill summaries can preserve a passing recommendation while surfacing model_runtime_changed
pinned runtime policy blocks mismatches with model_runtime_pinned_mismatch
improve proposals consume runtime-change context as a passing simplification reason
buildImproveInput assembles a bounded improvement packet from explicit evidence
generateImproveProposal turns explicit evidence into one bounded revision plan
buildRevisionArtifact materializes one durable revision packet from proposal and input
improve CLI helpers default inside active runs without losing packet boundaries
improve input, proposal, and revision artifact examples match checked-in schemas
report packet builder aggregates telemetry and scenario summaries
report packet builder preserves active-run and stdout fallback boundaries
report packet builder keeps packet-shaped inputs explicit

decision.reason: Focused app, runtime, and Node tests prove that improve build-artifact emits a reopenable revision artifact backed by checked-in builders, schemas, fixtures, and flow tests, while runtime fingerprint behavior records explicit machine-readable identity, preserves default observe-mode behavior, and blocks only declared pinned mismatches.
